title: Silky Jamaican Coconut Cake
description: A buttery, silky, romantic coconut cake infused with Jamaican spices, sweetened with natural sweetener for a diabetic-friendly treat.
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and line a 9‑inch round cake pan with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, whisk together coconut flour, almond flour, baking powder, baking soda, and sea salt.
In a separate bowl, beat the eggs, then stir in melted coconut oil, applesauce, vanilla, and coconut milk until smooth.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry ingredients and whisk until just combined; fold in the grated coconut and allulose.
Pour the batter into the prepared pan and tap gently to remove air bubbles.
Bake for 25‑30 minutes, or until the top is golden and a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Remove from oven and let the cake cool in the pan for 10 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool completely.
Optional: dust with powdered allulose or drizzle a thin layer of coconut cream for extra silkiness.
Slice and serve warm or at room temperature.
